title: [release-1.14] Add conditional release-checking system test
body:
Unfortunately on a number of occasions, Skopeo has been released officially with a `-dev` suffix in the version number.  Assist in catching this mistake at release time by the addition of a simple conditional test.  Note that it must be positively enabled by a magic env. var. before executing the system tests.

Original PR: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2631

(AI was used to help craft this PR)

comments.body: @lsm5 QQ: For this PR (and [this one](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2643) and [this one](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2644)) do I also need to backport the TMT changes or does that (and related) automation only function from `main`?

Short answer: no need for TMT changes upstream on these branches, assuming we're cool with Cirrus for now.

I moved the [release-1.18 branch to TMT](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2619) earlier as I saw that on RHEL z-streams sometime ago, but the other branches for which you created PRs are still on Cirrus here.

So, any TMT changes to these branches won't have any effect until we do the same enablement TMT here. And I prefer not to do any further TMT enablement until we find the right home for RHEL branches.

RE: changes in RHEL dist-git, you can copy over the TMT enablement from the c9s and c10s branches for [skopeo centos-stream](https://gitlab.com/redhat/centos-stream/rpms/skopeo/) and make the adjustment there.

> Original PR: #2631
> 
> (AI was used to help craft this PR)

@cevich btw, what AI exactly was used here? I guess it was only used for the backport? I don't see it mentioned on the original PR. Is it something that could be hooked up to upstream?

> So, any TMT changes to these branches won't have any effect until we do the same enablement TMT here. And I prefer not to do any further TMT enablement until we find the right home for RHEL branches.

Okay great, and thanks for the details.  So it does operate on a branch-by-branch basis.  Thanks for the RHEL dist-git references.

> btw, what AI exactly was used here? 

Oh nothing fancy.  I used [Cursor](https://cursor.com/en) to backport the commit adding the test, skip the TMT stuff, and do some minor massaging of the commit message.  It didn't touch any code.
